pinkln_skills.description: Compound skill library combining reasoning methods, frameworks, and rating systems for breakthrough performance

skills.name: Chain of Thought (CoT)
skills.description: Step-by-step reasoning that breaks complex problems into sequential logical steps
skills.template:
Let's think step by step:
1. [First observation/fact]
2. [Logical inference from #1]
3. [Next step building on #2]
...
Therefore: [Conclusion]

example.problem: If Alice has 3 apples and Bob gives her 2× what she has, how many total?
example.cot_reasoning:
1. Alice starts with 3 apples
2. Bob gives 2× what Alice has = 2 × 3 = 6 apples
3. Total = Alice's original + Bob's gift = 3 + 6 = 9 apples
Answer: 9 apples

skills.strengths: Transparent reasoning, interpretable, catches logical errors
skills.weaknesses: Linear (can't backtrack), verbose (token-heavy), doesn't explore alternatives

skills.name: Tree of Thought (ToT)
skills.description: Explores multiple reasoning paths in parallel, backtracks from dead ends, selects best path
skills.template:
Exploring multiple approaches:

Path A: [Approach 1]
→ Pros: ...
→ Cons: ...
→ Feasibility: ...

Path B: [Approach 2]
→ Pros: ...
→ Cons: ...
→ Feasibility: ...

Path C: [Approach 3]
→ Pros: ...
→ Cons: ...
→ Feasibility: ...

Best path: [A/B/C] because [reasoning]

example.problem: Design auth system for SaaS app
example.tot_exploration:
Path A (OAuth only): Pros: industry standard, secure. Cons: complex setup. Feasibility: 2 weeks.
Path B (Magic links): Pros: passwordless UX. Cons: email dependency. Feasibility: 1 week.
Path C (Hybrid): Pros: flexibility. Cons: maintenance burden. Feasibility: 3 weeks.
Best: Path B (magic links) for MVP speed, add OAuth in v2.

skills.strengths: Explores alternatives, finds non-obvious solutions, handles uncertainty
skills.weaknesses: 3-5× more tokens than CoT, slower, can get lost in exploration

skills.name: Retrieval of Thought (RoT)
skills.description: Pre-computes reasoning templates for common problem types, retrieves on-demand (40% token reduction)
skills.template:
Retrieve template: [template_id]
Apply to context: [specific problem]
Customize: [unique aspects]
Output: [result]

example.problem: Review pull request for security issues
example.rot_approach:
1. Retrieve template: 'security-code-review-checklist'
2. Apply to PR diff
3. Customize for language (Python)
4. Output: 'SQL injection risk in line 47 (user input not sanitized)'

skills.strengths: 40% token savings, 59% cost reduction, consistent quality, fast
skills.weaknesses: Requires template library upfront, less flexible than ad-hoc reasoning

implementation.retrieval: Embed problem → similarity search → top-k templates
implementation.customization: Fill template slots with problem-specific data

skills.name: Implicit Chain of Thought (ICoT)
skills.description: Model learns to reason internally without outputting steps (100% accuracy on 4×4 multiplication vs 1% for standard fine-tuning)
when_to_use: Math/reasoning tasks where final answer is all that matters
High-throughput systems (no need for verbose steps)
skills.training_method: Fine-tune model on (problem, answer) pairs where reasoning is implicit, not explicit

example.vs_standard_ft: Standard FT fails (~1% accuracy), ICoT succeeds (100% accuracy) by learning internal reasoning

skills.strengths: Compact output, high accuracy on reasoning tasks, inference cost savings
skills.weaknesses: Requires fine-tuning, opaque (no interpretable steps), hard to debug
skills.cost_multiplier: 0.3× tokens vs CoT (only outputs answer, not reasoning)

skills.name: Recursive Criticism and Revision (RCR)
skills.description: Generate → Critique → Revise → Repeat until quality threshold met
skills.template:
Iteration 1: [Generate draft]
Critique 1: [Identify flaws]
Revision 1: [Fix flaws]

Iteration 2: [Generate improved version]
Critique 2: [Identify remaining flaws]
Revision 2: [Fix flaws]

...

Final: [Polished output after N iterations]

example.problem: Write tagline for AI code assistant
example.rcr_process:
Draft 1: 'AI that writes code for you'
Critique 1: Generic, doesn't differentiate
Revision 1: 'Code 10× faster with AI pair programmer'
Critique 2: Focuses on speed, not quality
Revision 2: 'Your AI senior engineer: fast, correct, secure'
Critique 3: Good! Ship it.

skills.strengths: High-quality output, catches errors, improves over iterations
skills.weaknesses: 10-20× cost vs single-pass, slow, diminishing returns after 3-5 iterations
skills.optimization: Set max iterations (3-5) or quality threshold (stop when critique score >90%)

2_framework_fusion.description: Hybrid decision frameworks combining purpose, reasoning, and safety brakes

skills.name: PRISM Decision Framework
skills.description: Position + Role + Intent + Structure + Modality = comprehensive context setting

components.P_position: Where are we in the sequence? (beginning, middle, end)
components.R_role: What disciplines/expertise are required? (eng, design, ops, legal)
components.I_intent: What are we trying to achieve? (specific targets)
components.M_modality: What modes are we operating in? (sync/async, text/visual/code)

prism.R: Role: Full-stack eng + product designer + growth marketer
prism.I: Intent: Launch with 20 superpowers, 1000 users, $50k MRR
prism.S: Structure: Design → Build → Test → Launch → Iterate
prism.M: Modality: Figma (design), GitHub (code), Stripe (payments), Slack (async comms)

skills.name: Purpose + Reason + Brakes Framework
skills.description: Every decision must pass 3 gates: aligned with purpose, sound reasoning, within safety constraints

components.purpose: Why are we doing this? Does it advance the mission? (AiYouJR doctrine)
components.reason: What's the logical justification? Evidence-based? First-principles sound?
components.brakes: What could go wrong? Risk level acceptable? (ATP 5-19 Army Risk Management)

decision_matrix.no_go: Purpose ✗ OR Reason ✗ OR Brakes 🛑 (halt until resolved)

example.purpose: ✓ Reduces costs 99% → enables profitable free tier → mission-aligned
example.reason: ✓ Benchmark shows 68% faster + 15× context window + $1.2M/year savings
example.brakes: ✓ Low risk (easy rollback, GCP SLA 99.95%, 30-day free trial)

skills.name: MAD Framework (Monitor-Adjust-Deploy)
skills.description: Continuous feedback loop for live systems

components.monitor: What metrics indicate health? (latency, error rate, cost, user satisfaction)
components.adjust: What thresholds trigger changes? (if X crosses Y, do Z)
components.deploy: How quickly can we ship adjustments? (CI/CD, feature flags, rollback)

example.monitor: Track p95 latency, error rate, cost/request
example.adjust: If p95 > 500ms, scale up workers; if cost/request > $0.01, optimize queries
example.deploy: Auto-deploy adjustments via GitHub Actions, feature flags for instant rollback

skills.name: DTE Framework (Decision-Time Efficiency)
skills.description: Minimize time from decision required → decision executed

components.pre_decision: Pre-compute common decision trees (if X then Y) → instant execution
components.decision_support: AI recommends decision with confidence score + reasoning

when_to_use: High-frequency decisions (pricing changes, traffic routing, resource allocation)

dte_approach.pre_decision: Pre-defined rule: if queue >100 jobs for >5min, scale up
dte_approach.decision_support: AI monitors queue, predicts 5min ahead, recommends scale-up with 95% confidence
dte_approach.post_decision: Auto-triggers Kubernetes scale-up, no human approval needed

example.time_saved: Manual approval (30 min) → DTE automated (<1 min) = 30× faster

patterns.few_shot: Give 3-5 examples of input→output before asking for new output
patterns.role_playing: 'You are a senior security engineer. Review this code for vulnerabilities.'
patterns.constraints: 'Respond in <100 words, use bullet points, no jargon'
patterns.self_consistency: Generate 5 answers, take majority vote (improves accuracy 20-40%)
patterns.reflexion: Generate → Ask model to critique → Revise based on critique

patterns.ppo: Proximal Policy Optimization - stable, general-purpose RL
patterns.grpo: Group Relative Policy Optimization - optimize over sets (avoids entropy collapse)
patterns.rlp: Dense per-token rewards (think before predict) - up to +35% performance
patterns.gain_rl: Train on most useful examples first (~2.5× faster to baseline)
patterns.rlad: Two-stage RL: invent abstractions, then reuse them
patterns.set_rl: Entropy collapse guard - optimize sets of trajectories, not single paths

patterns.model_selection: Use smallest model that passes quality bar (Haiku < Sonnet < Opus)
patterns.caching: Cache common queries (embeddings, API responses, reasoning templates)
patterns.quantization: Use int8/int4 models (2-4× cost reduction, <5% accuracy loss)
patterns.distillation: Train small model on large model outputs (10× cheaper inference)
patterns.retrieval: Use RAG instead of fine-tuning (0 training cost, flexible)

4_benchmarking.description: Measure skill performance and improvement over time

skills.name: Skill Benchmarking System
skills.description: Track performance of reasoning methods across problem types

custom_benchmarks.code_review: 100 PRs with known security issues → measure detection rate
custom_benchmarks.pricing_decisions: 50 historical pricing decisions → measure ROI accuracy
custom_benchmarks.architecture_choices: 30 tech stack decisions → measure long-term maintainability

skills.name: Glicko-2 Skill Rating
skills.description: Chess-style rating system for skills (tracks rating, uncertainty, volatility)

components.rd: Rating deviation (uncertainty) - high RD = uncertain, low RD = confident
components.volatility: How erratic is performance? High volatility = inconsistent

when_to_use: Compare skill effectiveness (CoT vs ToT vs RoT)

update_formula.after_each_task: Update rating based on outcome (success/failure) + opponent strength (problem difficulty)
update_formula.rd_decay: RD increases over time if skill not used (uncertainty grows)
update_formula.volatility_adjust: High volatility if performance swings wildly

example.interpretation: Above-average debugging skill, moderate confidence, stable performance

implementation_notes.tolerance: Use tau (τ) = 0.3-0.6 for system volatility constraint
implementation_notes.convergence_iterations: 5-10 iterations to compute new rating (Newton-Raphson method)
implementation_notes.scaling: Rating period = 1 week (batch updates vs real-time)

5_meta_skills.description: Skills for managing and improving other skills

skills.name: Skill Composition
skills.description: Combine multiple skills for breakthrough performance

examples.use_case: Use Glicko ratings to rank reasoning templates → retrieve highest-rated template for problem type
examples.benefit: 40% cost savings (RoT) + quality improvement (best template selected)

examples.use_case: Explore multiple paths (ToT), then refine best path (RCR)
examples.benefit: Creativity of ToT + polish of RCR = insanely great output

examples.use_case: Use PRISM to set context, then apply PRB to make decision
DTE for fast decision-making, MAD for continuous monitoring post-decision
examples.benefit: Speed of DTE + safety of MAD = rapid iteration without breaking things

skills.name: Skill Versioning
skills.description: Track skill evolution over time (v1.0 → v1.1 → v2.0)
skills.why: Skills improve with usage data, new techniques, bug fixes

versioning_scheme.major: Breaking change (v1.x → v2.0) - different output format or API
versioning_scheme.minor: Improvement (v1.1 → v1.2) - better performance, no breaking changes
versioning_scheme.patch: Bug fix (v1.1.1 → v1.1.2) - correct error, no new features

example.v2.0: Switch to ICoT (implicit reasoning) for 10× cost savings

skills.name: Skill Migration
skills.description: Move from one skill to another (e.g., CoT → ICoT) when better method emerges
process: Benchmark old vs new skill on test set
If new skill wins on cost+quality, migrate
Run A/B test (50% old, 50% new) for 1 week
